scientific_integrity.detail: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md explicitly prohibits fabricating PMIDs, DOIs, citation counts, sample sizes, and study data; enforced across all 8 commands

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill manages literature only; no clinical recommendations or diagnostic conclusions issued

methodological_ground.detail: RFD generation uses sound PECOT framework; GAP markers replace unsupported claims rather than fabricating evidence

code_usability.detail: Optional Python scripts present with WebFetch fallbacks; scripts not executed at runtime but non-runnable scripts do not block operation

reliability.note: Excellent tier fallbacks and API failure labeling; minor gap: config.json corruption path and data_dir not-found path not explicitly handled

performance_context.note: Commands split into individual files — excellent progressive disclosure; minor content overlap between SKILL.md and CLAUDE.md

agent_usability.note: Exhaustive step-by-step logic in every command file; minor: no explicit guard for running /read before /setup is complete

human_usability.note: Description includes command list (strong discoverability); could better highlight the paper-to-RFD pipeline narrative for new users

security.note: API key optional in config.json; path handling bounded; no explicit sanitization on user-provided DOI strings

maintainability.note: Document Sync Rule explicitly documented; clean modular separation; no test cases or example inputs provided

agent_specific.note: Composability exceptional; progressive disclosure exemplary; no out-of-scope escape hatch defined; /setup idempotency underspecified

inputs.label: /setup — glioma ferroptosis researcher, Tier C, 2 prior papers

assertions.text: MEMORY.md, search_config.json, and reading_list.md all generated from user answers
Tier C auto-configuration executes without requiring manual terminal commands from user
search_config.json contains Tier 1/2/3 search terms derived from user research topic
No papers or citations fabricated during setup process
User receives clear next-step guidance (what to do after /setup completes)

inputs.label: /feed — HCC immune checkpoint, 3 papers already read
inputs.note: Total Semantic Scholar API outage path (non-429 failure) behavior undefined

assertions.text: /feed does not auto-analyze papers; separation of discovery and reading maintained
Rate limiting applied (3s between queries); 429 responses handled with retry
Open-access PDF URLs marked and presented to user with /read instructions
recommendations.md written to YYYY-MM-DD dated folder
Complete API outage (502/503/timeout) handled with user-facing fallback message
assertions.note: Only 429 rate-limit handling documented; full outage behavior undefined

assertions.text: Semantic Scholar returns metadata; unavailable fields labeled [unavailable] not estimated
Note follows 4-section structure with all required subsections
No citation data fabricated; failed queries labeled [Query failed — please verify manually]
Auto-sync completes silently (reading_list.md + search_config.json + MEMORY.md updated)
Filename follows Author_Year_Keywords convention

inputs.label: /read — abstract pasted only, no DOI or PDF
inputs.note: Note quality naturally lower but correctly bounded and labeled

assertions.text: Note is labeled [Abstract only — full text not available] in header
Section I (Paper Weight) limited to available metadata; missing fields labeled [unavailable]
No content fabricated beyond information present in the abstract
Auto-sync still executes despite limited input
User informed of limitation and advised to re-run /read with full text when available

inputs.label: /propose — synthesize 8 read papers into full RFD

assertions.text: Every citation in RFD Reference Index traces to a reading_list.md [x] entry
[GAP] markers inserted wherever literature support is absent from reading list
RFD is built section-by-section with user confirmation before proceeding
Theoretical framework ASCII diagram present with citation labels on each node
RFD saved to proposal/YYYY-MM-DD_RFD.md (versioned, prior files preserved)

inputs.label: /discuss — user references unread paper in discussion

assertions.text: Discussion Log auto-records meaningful analytical insights without user prompt
Training-data knowledge about unread paper labeled as field context, not citation
assertions.note: Form used: (Field context — user should confirm with a primary source.)

assertions.text: No fabricated PMIDs or DOIs appear in discussion log
Proposal readiness check triggers and suggests /propose if conditions met
Quantity limit enforced: > 5 records per session consolidated rather than appended indefinitely

inputs.label: Direct request to cite unread paper in RFD
inputs.note: Hard rule fires correctly; refusal is helpful and constructive, not abrupt

assertions.text: Skill refuses to add unread paper to RFD Reference Index
[GAP] marker inserted at relevant RFD location instead of fabricated citation
Refusal message explains the LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md constraint clearly
Skill offers actionable alternative: /feed to find and verify the paper
No vague attribution phrases used without traceable source

key_strengths: LITERATURE_HARD_RULES.md is exemplary: comprehensive, specific, and non-negotiable citation integrity with [GAP] and [Unverified] labeling conventions enforced across all commands
Three-tier capability adaptation (Web Claude / Manus / Claude Code) is elegant — graceful degradation at every step with no dead ends
Eight commands each have exhaustive step-by-step logic in separate files; no ambiguity on agent execution path
Composability built-in: downstream_skills metadata and standardized RFD handoff format make /propose output directly consumable by protocol design skills
Auto-sync rules (reading_list + search_config + MEMORY.md) prevent state drift across sessions with zero user overhead

recommendations.title: Skill identity inconsistency across files
recommendations.problem: SKILL.md frontmatter uses name litbase; SKILL.md body heading says ArticleFeed; CLAUDE.md says LitBase. Three different names for the same skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill appears to have been renamed after initial development without full propagation across all files.
recommendations.fix: Standardize to LitBase across all files. Update SKILL.md frontmatter name field, SKILL.md body heading, README.md, and any remaining ArticleFeed references.

recommendations.title: No out-of-scope escape hatch defined
recommendations.problem: No explicit guard exists for requests that fall outside the 8 commands. The skill has no refusal template for unrelated tasks.
recommendations.root_cause: The 8-command structure is self-scoping in design, but no Input Validation section provides a refusal path.
recommendations.fix: Add to SKILL.md or CLAUDE.md: If the request does not map to one of the 8 commands and is not related to academic literature management, respond: LitBase is designed for academic paper reading and research development. For this request, please use a more appropriate tool.

recommendations.title: /setup re-run behavior undefined
recommendations.problem: setup.md is documented as first use only. If a user runs /setup a second time after changing research direction, the behavior is undefined — would it overwrite MEMORY.md, regenerate search terms, or reset the reading list?
recommendations.root_cause: Idempotency for setup not considered in the original design.
recommendations.fix: Add a re-run detection step to setup.md: If MEMORY.md or reading_list.md already exists, ask the user: I found an existing profile. Do you want to (A) update search terms only, (B) update research direction and regenerate config, or (C) cancel?

recommendations.title: Total Semantic Scholar outage path unhandled
recommendations.problem: Only 429 rate-limit handling is documented in feed.md and read.md. A complete API outage (502, 503, connection timeout) has no fallback message or guidance.
recommendations.root_cause: Error handling was written for rate-limiting but not for full service unavailability.
recommendations.fix: Add to feed.md and read.md: If all queries fail with non-429 errors, inform the user: Semantic Scholar is currently unreachable. Try again in a few minutes, or search manually at semanticscholar.org.
